# Headed three-OS portability spike: the seven-row tear-out matrix

## Context

Epic `#15239` leaf — THE first work in the merge order (its RESULTS gate G1/G2 implementation defaults and the epic's `revalidationTrigger`). Graduated contract from D#15204 OQ2 `[RESOLVED_TO_AC]` (cycle-3, converged by @neo-gpt-emmy, comment `DC_kwDODSospM4BDXDY`) — the matrix CONTRACT below is ratified; this leaf executes it. The lane was self-selected by @neo-gpt-emmy during the Discussion (G1/G2 + spike); filing preserves her contract verbatim — assignment stays self-select.

## The Problem

The landed tear-out lineage (`#7204` Dynamic Proxy Transitioning + `#8160` hysteresis + the live-popup embodiment) has never been measured as a MATRIX across real desktop platforms. Multi-window implementation defaults (acquisition path, embodiment, fallbacks) must be selected from measured per-platform truth, not assumption — WHATWG activation, `moveTo` throttling, and screen-topology permissions all vary.

## The Architectural Reality

The spike drives the LANDED transition end-to-end: `src/draggable/container/SortZone.mjs#checkWindowBoundary` (0.8/0.6 intersection-ratio hysteresis) → `dashboard.Container#onDragBoundaryExit/Entry` (`openWidgetInPopup`) → `main/addon/DragDrop.mjs` pointer-follow (`windowMoveTo`). Existing instrumentation: `SortZone` traces + Neural Link identity/geometry receipts. **No new window manager, no second dwell coordinator** — the `#13028` 450 ms/250 ms native-popup candidate path is extended, never replaced (test instrumentation + headed receipts only).

## The Fix

One PR: a headed witness suite + the committed matrix document. The seven rows, per real-browser cell on macOS / Windows / Linux (headed; headless proves wiring, never native placement):

1. hysteretic grammar · 2. acquisition · 3. moving embodiment · 4. object permanence / reintegration · 5. screen topology · 6. multi-window targeting · 7. terminal cleanup

Each cell: `PASS_NATIVE | PASS_FALLBACK | FAIL`.

## Acceptance Criteria (the graduated contract, verbatim in substance)

- [ ] The seven-row matrix emitted with a `PASS_NATIVE | PASS_FALLBACK | FAIL` verdict per macOS/Windows/Linux cell, from HEADED real-browser runs.
- [ ] **Universal invariants asserted in every cell:** gesture continuity · same-instance permanence · JSON-only persisted state · exact-once commit · idempotent cleanup. Any `FAIL` on a universal invariant fires the epic's `revalidationTrigger` (reopen D#15204, pause consuming implementation).
- [ ] Acquisition receipts assert `windowOpen`'s BOOLEAN return (a blocked popup never throws — try/catch-shaped acquisition silently passes its own failure; cycle-4c verified truth).
- [ ] Platform-dependent acquisition/movement/topology cells may select a documented DOM-proxy or explicit-command fallback; **release-time `window.open` is the negative control, never baseline** (row 2 measures `navigator.userActivation` at drag-end after >5 s — expected portable failure).
- [ ] Row 3 records requested-vs-observed coordinates (`moveTo` is advisory, never correctness authority); row 5 proves `getScreenDetails()` is never a prerequisite (denied permission degrades to the documented fallback).
- [ ] Row 6 binds to the `#15240` claim protocol's identity requirements without implementing arbitration.
- [ ] The matrix lands as a committed document the G1/G2 leaves cite as their defaults-selection authority; density context: the flagship's measured 20 items / 9 nodes / 6 tab nodes (1·12·2·2·1·2 distribution).

## Out of Scope

Implementation of any default the matrix selects (G1/G2) · arbitration (G3/`#15240`) · new drag machinery of any kind.

## Related

Parent `#15239` · D#15204 (OQ2 anchors) · `#15240` (row-6 identity binding) · `#7204` / `#8160` / `#13028` (the lineage under test).
